Politics.headline: White House says Donald Trump 'did nothing wrong' amid reports he showed classified map on plane in 2022 – US politics live
Politics.detail: The White House defended Donald Trump amid reports he showed off a classified map on a 2022 flight, and also held onto a sensitive record from his first term that only a few people had access to. A top House Democrat has released a letter adding to public scrutiny of Trump's retention of classified documents.

Politics.headline: California governor's race gets weirder with debate cancellation, new poll
Politics.detail: A debate at the University of Southern California was canceled amid racism allegations because the invited candidates were white, while excluded candidates were Latino, Black, or Asian. A new poll confirms the leading candidates are Republicans Steve Hilton and Chad Bianco, while Democrats trail.

Politics.headline: Youth Select Committee publishes report on PSHE education
Politics.detail: The Youth Select Committee in the UK released a report on PSHE (Personal, Social, Health and Economic) education, finding inconsistencies in quality and teacher preparedness. The committee examined PSHE in secondary schools, exploring consistency, teacher support, and how political education can prepare young people for a potential lowering of the voting age to 16.

Politics.headline: China opposes foreign interference, supports Venezuela's sovereignty
Politics.detail: China's Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Lin Jian stated China's firm opposition to forcibly seizing a country's president, emphasizing it violates international law. Lin Jian also reiterated China's opposition to political manipulation under the pretext of “foreign interference” allegations.

Politics.headline: EU to become founding member of Special Tribunal for Crime of Aggression against Ukraine
Politics.detail: The European Commission has adopted a proposal for the European Union to become a founding member of the Special Tribunal for the Crime of Aggression against Ukraine. The EU aims to play a central role in the Tribunal's operations, including membership in the Management Committee.

Finance & Economy.headline: FCA plans to help people get more financial advice for important decisions
Finance & Economy.detail: The Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) in the UK is planning new initiatives to help individuals access financial advice for critical decisions, ensuring they are better informed.

Finance & Economy.headline: Small businesses in Canada facing 'big' labour shortages
Finance & Economy.detail: Canadian small businesses are struggling with significant labour shortages, impacting their operations and growth.

Finance & Economy.headline: GM to invest US$600 million in its South Korean unit
Finance & Economy.detail: General Motors (GM) is set to invest US$600 million in its South Korean unit, reinforcing its commitment to the region.

Finance & Economy.headline: Gold Rate Today 25th March 2026: Gold is up by 4.37%
Finance & Economy.detail: Today, March 25, 2026, gold prices have increased by 4.37% in major Indian cities like Mumbai, Chennai, Delhi, and Bengaluru.

Finance & Economy.headline: Silver Rate Today 25th March 2026: Silver is up by 5.59%
Finance & Economy.detail: Silver prices have seen a rise of 5.59% today, March 25, 2026, across key cities in India including Mumbai, Bengaluru, Hyderabad, Delhi, and Ahmedabad.

Technology.headline: Microsoft Aims to Supercharge Windows Performance and Cut RAM Waste in 2026
Technology.detail: Microsoft is working on improvements to Windows that aim to improve performance and reduce RAM usage, addressing some of the biggest pain points for users.

Technology.headline: Arm unveils new AI chip, expects it to add billions in annual revenue
Technology.detail: Arm has announced a new AI chip and anticipates it will generate billions in annual revenue, marking a significant step for the company in the artificial intelligence sector.

Technology.headline: Samsung Galaxy S26 Is Getting AirDrop Support For Quick Share Starting March 23
Technology.detail: The Samsung Galaxy S26 will feature AirDrop support through Quick Share, starting March 23, allowing for easier file sharing between Samsung devices.

Technology.headline: HP stuffs OpenAI LLM into new laptops to make them either more useful at work, or a bit creepy
Technology.detail: HP is integrating OpenAI's LLM into its new laptops, offering features like chat, file sharing, and meeting summaries, raising questions about utility and privacy.

Technology.headline: OnePlus confirms Nord 6 with Snapdragon 8s Gen 4 and 9,000mAh battery coming to India on April 7
Technology.detail: OnePlus has confirmed the launch of the Nord 6 in India on April 7, featuring a Snapdragon 8s Gen 4 processor and a substantial 9,000mAh battery.

Science.headline: Scientists Discover Pathway That Activates Brown Fat Could Lead to New Obesity Treatment
Science.detail: Researchers have discovered a pathway that activates brown fat, potentially leading to new treatments for obesity.

Science.headline: High-Resolution Atlas of Developing Human Brain Combines Data from Nearly 200 Studies and 30 Million Cells
Science.detail: A new high-resolution atlas of the developing human brain combines data from nearly 200 studies and 30 million cells, offering unprecedented insights into brain development.

Science.headline: Star's Mysterious X-Rays Finally Explained After 50 Years
Science.detail: After 50 years, scientists have finally uncovered the source of a star's unusually powerful X-rays using precise observations from Japan's XRISM space telescope.

Science.headline: Earth's Days Are Getting Longer, And Humans May Be Why
Science.detail: A recent study suggests that Earth's days are getting longer, potentially influenced by human activities.

Science.headline: Scientists have discovered that losing a key protein in small cell lung cancer triggers inflammation that actually helps tumors grow and spread.
Science.detail: Losing a key protein in small cell lung cancer triggers inflammation that actually helps tumors grow and spread, and it pushes cancer cells into a more aggressive, neuron-like state.

Health.headline: Digital CBT reduced cardiac-related anxiety and improved health following heart attack
Health.detail: A new study published in the Journal of the American College of Cardiology shows that digital CBT treatment reduced cardiac-related anxiety and improved patients' quality of life and physical function after a heart attack, compared to standard care. The study involved 96 people who had a heart attack at least six months prior and experienced cardiac-related anxiety.

Health.headline: Two thirds of consultants want to work for new NHS online hospital
Health.detail: A poll reveals that 60% of NHS consultants are interested in working for the new NHS online hospital, which aims to provide faster specialist care through virtual appointments and assessments. The service is expected to deliver up to 8.5 million virtual appointments in its first three years and will treat conditions like menopause and prostate problems, with recurring urinary tract infections and suspected polycystic ovaries being added.

Health.headline: Opinion: A world of medical hurt ahead
Health.detail: Due to Congress not sustaining ACA subsidies, millions of Americans could face dangerous health system damages such as 750 rural hospitals closing, two thirds of ACA recipients reducing spending on clothing and food, and medical debt becoming their primary financial burden. Uninsured adults may skip screenings and treatments, leading to increased risk of stroke, heart attack, blindness, amputations and kidney failure.

Health.headline: Study reveals COVID-19 stress linked to increased substance use during pregnancy
Health.detail: A study published in Frontiers in Public Health found that stress related to the COVID-19 pandemic was associated with higher use of tobacco and cannabis among pregnant women. The research, which collected data from over 3,200 pregnant participants during the first year of the pandemic, also revealed that psychological distress from worrying about health and financial instability contributed to substance use.

Health.headline: NHS reform and the future of the health service
Health.detail: Health and Social Care Secretary, Wes Streeting spoke in London today on the steps being taken to reform the health service.

Sports.headline: Local Sports Recap – March 25, 2026
Sports.detail: The Carmi-White County Baseball team lost to Hardin County with a final score of 5-1, while the Carmi-White County Softball team fell to Carterville with a final score of 13-1. Both teams have upcoming games scheduled.

Sports.headline: Guangdong Launches Grassroots Basketball League as Local Sports Culture Draws Strong Public Interest
Sports.detail: Guangdong, China has launched a new provincial grassroots basketball tournament, called Guangdong B.A., designed exclusively for amateur players to showcase local talent and increase community participation; the inaugural tournament began on March 21 at Guangzhou's Tianhe Sports Center.

Sports.headline: 2026 World Match Racing Tour – Macao Match Cup sets sail today
Sports.detail: The Macao Match Cup, the opening event of the 2026 World Match Racing Tour (WMRT), began today and will continue for five days, with 12 elite sailing teams competing for the championship. After the first day, France's Aurélien Pierroz is the only undefeated skipper in the competition.

Sports.headline: Nikola Jokic has 23 points, knocks down late bucket to send Nuggets over Suns 125-123
Sports.detail: Nikola Jokic scored 23 points, including a late basket, to lead the Denver Nuggets to a 125-123 victory over the Phoenix Suns. Mitchell scored 42 as Cavaliers defeat Magic 136-131 to extend winning streak to 4 games.

Sports.headline: Warriors' Moses Moody suffers season-ending knee injury
Sports.detail: Moses Moody's gruesome injury deeply disturbed the Warriors and he is out for the season. The Warriors have faced heartbreak after heartbreak this season.

Entertainment.headline: Bill Cosby Found Liable in Sexual Assault Case, Owes Victim $59M
Entertainment.detail: Bill Cosby is ordered to pay $59 million in damages to Donna Motsinger for drugging and assaulting her in 1972.

Entertainment.headline: JAY-Z Breaks Silence on Being Accused in Diddy Lawsuits
Entertainment.detail: JAY-Z has addressed the allegations of sexual misconduct tied to Sean 'Diddy' Combs, in a recent 'GQ' interview.

Entertainment.headline: Tom Holland Declares Zendaya 'Looks Amazing' as They Step Out in Paris Amid Marriage Speculation
Entertainment.detail: Tom Holland praised Zendaya's appearance as they appeared in Paris, amidst rumors of a secret wedding.

Entertainment.headline: A woman prosecutors say fired shots at Rihanna's home in attempt to kill her set to appear in court
Entertainment.detail: A woman who prosecutors say fired shots at Rihanna's home while the singer and her family were there is set to appear in court.

Entertainment.headline: Chuck Norris Dead at 86
Entertainment.detail: Action icon Chuck Norris has reportedly passed away at the age of 86.

Crime & Justice.headline: Ex-Tory minister Crispin Blunt pleads guilty to possession of crystal meth
Crime & Justice.detail: Former prisons minister Crispin Blunt has pleaded guilty to four charges of possession of drugs including cannabis and crystal meth, which stemmed from a raid on his home in Surrey in October 2023.

Crime & Justice.headline: Judge sanctions public defender in case that raises critical issues about criminal justice
Crime & Justice.detail: A San Francisco judge has ordered Public Defender Mano Raju to pay $26,000 in fines for refusing to take cases that his office can't handle because of crushing workloads, with public defenders arguing that legal ethics and constitutional law require attorneys to have the capacity to represent their clients effectively.

Crime & Justice.headline: Sleaford - prolific shoplifter sent to prison
Crime & Justice.detail: Nathan Stevenson, a 32-year-old with no fixed address, has been sentenced to 28 weeks in prison after pleading guilty to five counts of theft from a shop and five counts of breaching a Criminal Behaviour Order (CBO) in Sleaford between 12 – 18 March.

Crime & Justice.headline: US Justice Department lacks evidence of wrongdoing by Fed chair, official testifies
Crime & Justice.detail: The US Justice Department has found no evidence that Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell committed any crimes related to the $2.5 billion renovation of its Washington headquarters, according to a senior deputy to the US attorney for the District of Columbia.

Crime & Justice.headline: Judge fines San Francisco public defender after finding him in contempt for refusing new cases
Crime & Justice.detail: A judge has fined San Francisco's public defender $26,000 for ignoring an order to stop rejecting cases, a pattern that critics say is straining the city's justice system due to crushing workloads and insufficient staffing.

Education.headline: Perfect homework, blank stares: Why colleges are turning to oral exams to combat AI
Education.detail: A growing number of U.S. college instructors are turning to oral exams to help combat an AI crisis in higher education.

Education.headline: Georgia could become the first state with weapons detection in all public schools
Education.detail: Georgia is considering becoming the first state to implement weapons detection systems in all public schools to enhance safety.

Education.headline: Michigan pits small, big schools for funding. It's an unfair fight, leaders say
Education.detail: Leaders in Michigan argue that the state's school funding model unfairly disadvantages smaller schools when compared to larger institutions.

Education.headline: CT homeschool bill advances in split committee vote, despite vast opposition
Education.detail: A bill concerning homeschooling regulations in Connecticut has moved forward despite significant opposition, indicating a contentious debate over educational freedom and oversight.

Education.headline: Judge orders Texas to open school choice program to Islamic schools.
Education.detail: A judge has mandated that Texas include Islamic schools in its school choice program following legal challenges, ensuring religious diversity in educational opportunities.

Lifestyle.headline: Stars deliver modern and colorful looks on Oscars red carpet
Lifestyle.detail: Stars were decked out for the 98th annual Academy Awards, the final stop on Hollywood's monthslong awards season, showcasing modern and colorful styles.

Lifestyle.headline: Miley Cyrus channeled Y2K makeup for the Hannah Montana 20th anniversary special
Lifestyle.detail: Miley Cyrus paid tribute to her iconic character Hannah Montana with a Y2K-inspired makeup look for the show's 20th-anniversary special.

Lifestyle.headline: Holy parades and earthly pleasures in Spain: Easter in Granada
Lifestyle.detail: Granada, Spain, is celebrating Easter with holy parades and earthly pleasures.

Lifestyle.headline: A moment that changed me: I thought my Parkinson's was the end of my life, but dancing changed everything
Lifestyle.detail: A person shares how dancing helped them cope with their Parkinson's diagnosis and changed their life for the better.

Lifestyle.headline: Greater weight loss promised by higher-dose Wegovy shot, now approved by FDA
Lifestyle.detail: The FDA has approved Wegovy HD, a higher-dose semaglutide for weight loss in adults with obesity, offering greater weight reduction, though experts warn of potential side effects.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: America's rating in freedom and democracy continue to decline under Trump
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Despite the United States being a beacon for democracy and freedom, it continues to decline in Freedom House's annual rankings, with the U.S. receiving a 'B' grade, its lowest since reporting began. The report cites factors such as an escalation in executive dominance, legislative dysfunction, pressure on free expression, and the undermining of anti-corruption safeguards.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Guardian view on the Iran war: energy, markets and a dangerous illusion
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The US-Israel war on Iran has caused a dangerous illusion. The editorial board at the Guardian discusses the energy and market implications of the war.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: OPINION: Establishment Democrats in Colorado Face Voter Exasperation
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Younger, top Democratic challengers are emerging as Democratic candidates who base their campaigns solely on opposition to Trump are missing the point, according to a Denver political activist. A democratic socialist born in Ethiopia, offers a clear vision that...

Opinion/Editorial.headline: 'Sammy's Law' Important
Opinion/Editorial.detail: A bipartisan group of lawmakers are working on ways to better protect kids online by giving parents the right to receive safety notifications through Federal Trade Commission-regulated third-party providers. The bill would require alerts to parents when 15 specific instances or phrases arise that indicate eating disorders, suicidal ideation and sexual harassment.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: LFP Letters to the Editor: March 25, 2026
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Letters to the editor include topics such as the Liberal government's gun buyback program being an ineffective way to deal with gun violence and London needing more tennis courts, both indoor and outdoor, especially as demand far outstrips supply. A letter also discusses that encouraging and helping women participate in sports cultivates resilience, skilled and engagement, and these attributes apply to both women who choose a career in business and those who choose motherhood.

World.headline: US talking to itself, says Iran as Trump claims wheels of diplomacy turning
World.detail: Iranian officials continue to deny any peace talks, amid reports the US sent the country a ceasefire proposal. Iran dismisses Trump's claims of negotiations, stating the US is talking to itself.

World.headline: Russia, Ukraine tit-for-tat attacks knock out power for over half a million
World.detail: Attacks between Russia and Ukraine have resulted in power outages for approximately 450,000 people in the Belgorod region and 150,000 consumers in Chernihiv. The strikes showcase the ongoing conflict's impact on civilian infrastructure.

World.headline: Belarus's leader makes first visit to North Korea in bid to deepen ties
World.detail: Alexander Lukashenko, the Belarusian President, is visiting North Korea for the first time, with both countries expected to sign a treaty of friendship. This visit signals a deepening of relations between Belarus and North Korea.

World.headline: Israeli air attacks hit southern Lebanese towns.
World.detail: Israeli air attacks have struck towns in southern Lebanon. The attacks are part of the ongoing conflict in the region.

World.headline: US reportedly communicates 15-point cease-fire proposal with Iran
World.detail: The United States has reportedly communicated a 15-point ceasefire proposal to Iran. This comes amidst ongoing tensions and conflict in the region.

Zimbabwe.headline: President's devolution drive ignites hospitality boom in Maphisa
Zimbabwe.detail: The ongoing devolution efforts led by the President are spurring growth in the hospitality sector in Maphisa, reflecting broader economic development at the local level.

Zimbabwe.headline: TIMB pushes fair play as tobacco sales surge
Zimbabwe.detail: The Tobacco Industry and Marketing Board (TIMB) is emphasizing the importance of fair practices as tobacco sales increase, aiming to ensure equitable benefits for farmers and stakeholders.

Zimbabwe.headline: Marketing rep remanded over US$65k fraud
Zimbabwe.detail: A marketing representative has been remanded in custody following allegations of fraud involving US$65,000, highlighting ongoing concerns about financial irregularities.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zanu PF takes 2030 Bill campaign to Heroes Acre
Zimbabwe.detail: Zanu PF is campaigning for the 2030 Bill at Heroes Acre.

Zimbabwe.headline: US$4m exchange control case lands administrator in court
Zimbabwe.detail: A Harare-based administrator, Maud Patricia Liddell, appeared in court facing charges of violating exchange control regulations involving over US$4 million for allegedly externalizing funds from a property sale without Reserve Bank approval; she was granted bail and remanded to April 24.
